headline: Morgan Stanley Warns Arcos Dorados Holdings Inc. (ARCO) Earnings Miss Could Weigh on Stock Performance

headline: These 10 stocks are poised for earnings surprises in April: Morgan Stanley
summary: Investing.com-- Morgan Stanley is highlighting a fresh set of U.S. and European stocks with the strongest earnings surprise potential ahead of the upcoming reporting season, using a quantitative framework that blends analyst ratings with forward-looking signals. The bank’s approach combines metrics from its Earnings Forecast Landscape, Earnings Quality, and broader forecast dynamics to construct what it calls an Earnings Surprise Composite. headline: How Recent Analyst Shifts Are Rewriting The Story For TeamViewer (XTRA:TMV)
summary: The latest research round on TeamViewer leaves the central fair value unchanged at €8.57 per share. Fresh price targets now span a wide range from around €5 to above €8. That gap reflects a clear split in analyst opinion, with more cautious views leaning toward €5 after target cuts from levels such as €6.50, and more constructive calls still anchored closer to €8, even when nudged down from €8.35 to €8.25. As you read on, you will see what is driving this spread and how to track the narrative...

headline: The Bull Case For Morgan Stanley (MS) Could Change Following Its Expanding Wealth Role And SpaceX IPO Buzz
summary: Morgan Stanley’s recent headlines span strong wealth management milestones, upbeat earnings revisions, and expanding roles in capital markets, including a potential lead retail allocation for the anticipated SpaceX IPO via E*TRADE. Together with its presence at high-profile industry forums and fresh recognition from institutional investors, these developments underscore how Morgan Stanley is increasingly viewed as a fee-rich wealth and investment manager rather than a traditional Wall Street...

headline: How The Zebra Technologies (ZBRA) Investment Story Is Shifting With Conflicting Analyst Signals
summary: Zebra Technologies’ fair value price target has been adjusted to US$333.25 from US$336.38, a small move that signals a fine tuning rather than a reset. This shift comes as analysts weigh mixed research, with some raising targets into the low to high US$300s and others trimming estimates after concerns around Q3 and organic growth. As you read on, you will see how these changing targets fit into the broader story and what to watch as the narrative develops. Stay updated as the Fair Value for...
